title: Piquant Turkish Lentil Soup
description: A bright, herbaceous lentil soup with a creamy hummus swirl, baked al fresco for a touch of smokiness and served in a bento box for a convenient, plant-based lunch.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add onion, carrot, celery, and garlic; sauté until softened, about 5 minutes.
Stir in diced tomatoes, red lentils, and smoked paprika; cook for 2 minutes.
Pour in vegetable broth and water; bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 20 minutes until lentils are tender.
Use an immersion blender to partially blend the soup, creating a creamy texture, or transfer to a blender for a smoother consistency.
Stir in hummus until fully incorporated and heated through.
Season with salt, pepper, and lemon juice to taste.
Transfer the soup to a preheated, wood-fired oven tray and bake at 180°C for 10 minutes to develop a subtle smokiness; drizzle with a drizzle of olive oil before serving.
Garnish with chopped parsley and serve in a bento box.
